stations.label: BORN UNDER SPANISH FLAG
stations.line: Me pariรณ una colonia que ni siquiera me llamaba mรญa todavรญa — nazco con el nombre de Espaรฑa en la boca y el azรบcar en las venas.
stations.era_photo: A wet-plate collodion daguerreotype, sepia and scratched, of a woman in a mid-19th-century Havana dress standing beside a colonial treasury desk with early banknotes and Spanish reales, harsh studio lighting, long exposure blur at the edges of her skirt.

stations.label: TIED TO THE DOLLAR
stations.line: Ese aรฑo me casaron con el dรณlar sin preguntarme — uno a uno, decรญan, como si eso fuera igualdad y no una correa.
stations.era_photo: A silver gelatin black-and-white photograph, slightly overexposed, of a woman in a 1910s linen dress and wide hat on a Havana bank staircase, the first official peso bills fanned in her hand, formal and stiff-backed.

stations.label: THE YANKEE BANKS LEAVE
stations.line: Se fueron los bancos americanos con sus maletines y sus caras largas, y por primera vez fui mรญa — mรญa y sola, que no es lo mismo.
stations.era_photo: A grainy silver gelatin news photograph, high contrast, of a woman in a simple 1960 cotton dress standing in the empty marble lobby of a nationalized Havana bank, revolutionary posters half-hung behind her, cigarette in hand.

stations.label: THE EMBARGO SLAMS SHUT
stations.line: Cerraron la puerta y me dejaron adentro — sesenta aรฑos despuรฉs sigo parada en ese umbral, mirando pa'fuera, sin mover un pie.
stations.era_photo: A Kodachrome color photograph with saturated reds and faded blues, a woman in early-1960s dress leaning in a doorway of a Havana solar, peeling paint, tropical light flat and heavy, her expression unreadable.

stations.label: THE DOLLAR BECOMES LAW
stations.line: Aguantรฉ el Perรญodo Especial con hambre de museo, y luego legalizaron el dรณlar en mi propia casa — a mรญ me sentaron en la cocina mientras el yuma comรญa en la sala.
stations.era_photo: A faded 35mm color snapshot, orange cast and light leaks, a woman in a worn 1990s blouse standing in a bare Havana kitchen with empty ration shelves, holding a peso bill and a dollar bill in separate hands.

stations.label: SPLIT INTO TWO SELVES
stations.line: Me partieron en dos — el CUC pa'l turista y yo pa'l cubano — y aprendรญ a vivir con una versiรณn mรญa que valรญa veinticinco veces mรกs y no era yo.
stations.era_photo: An early digital photograph, slightly noisy, a woman standing between two casas de cambio windows in Havana, one marked CUC and one marked CUP, flat fluorescent light, her body angled toward the poorer window.

stations.label: THE UNIFICATION WOUND
stations.line: Mataron a mi otra mitad y me devolvieron entera de golpe — la Tarea Ordenamiento me dejรณ รบnica otra vez, con los precios volando y la gente contando centavos como quien reza.
stations.era_photo: A crisp smartphone photograph, natural window light, a woman in a 2021 mask pulled to her chin, standing in a long Havana line outside a store, holding a thick stack of devalued peso notes, tired but upright.

stations.label: STILL IN THE DOORWAY
stations.line: Aquรญ sigo, asere — con la inflaciรณn comiรฉndome viva y el MLC burlรกndose en la vidriera, pero de pie, siempre de pie, que caerse es un lujo que nunca me han vendido barato.
stations.era_photo: A high-resolution digital photograph, warm late-afternoon Malecรณn light, a woman in her working clothes leaning on the seawall with Havana crumbling behind her, waves breaking, her contralto almost audible in the frame.
